pstree_anomalies.anomaly_detected: Anomalous parent process or non-standard child process execution. Spawning of cmd.exe (PID 5120) which subsequently executed an unsigned binary in a temporary directory.
pstree_anomalies.details: Parent process services.exe (PID 840) is standard, but the child process tree contains suspicious command-line executions and shell spawning.

pstree_anomalies.anomaly_detected: Interactive shell spawned by a service host process without user session association.
Unsigned binary executing from a public user profile directory, spawned via an orphaned/service-spawned shell.
pstree_anomalies.details: File path: C:\Users\Public\Music\update.exe. Network sockets associated with this PID show active outbound connections to known malicious C2 infrastructure.

intent_analysis.justification: The presence of an interactive shell (cmd.exe) spawned by svchost.exe, which in turn executes an unsigned binary (update.exe) from a public directory (C:\Users\Public\Music\), combined with active outbound network connections to a known malicious IP address, confirms active threat actor presence and command-and-control (C2) beaconing. This is not a false positive or standard administrative activity.
mitigation_steps: Isolate the host from the network immediately.
Terminate process PID 5892 and associated parent trees.
Collect C:\Users\Public\Music\update.exe for static and dynamic malware analysis.
Block the remote IP address 185.220.101.5 at the perimeter firewall.
